children.name: Centurio
children.description: Centurio — a genus of new world leaf-nosed bats in the family Phyllostomidae.

children.name: Centurio senex
children.description: The wrinkle-faced bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae and the only identified member of the genus Centurio. This bat is found in various countries in and around Central America. It eats fruit but is not classified within the fruit bats, and is instead classified as a leaf-nosed bat even though it does not have a leaf nose.

children.name: Erophylla
children.description: Erophylla — a genus of new world leaf-nosed bats in the family Phyllostomidae.

children.name: Erophylla bombifrons
children.description: The brown flower bat is a species of bat from the family Phyllostomidae native to the island of Hispaniola, and Puerto Rico.

children.name: Erophylla sezekorni
children.description: The buffy flower bat is a species of bat in the leaf-nosed bat family, Phyllostomidae. It is found in the Bahamas, the Cayman Islands, Cuba, and Jamaica.

children.name: Scleronycteris
children.description: Scleronycteris — a genus of new world leaf-nosed bats in the family Phyllostomidae.

children.name: Scleronycteris ega
children.description: The Ega long-tongued bat is a bat species found in northwestern Brazil and southern Venezuela. It is monotypic within its genus. It is a rare species that has only been confirmed in four localities.

children.name: Uroderma
children.description: Uroderma — a genus of new world leaf-nosed bats in the family Phyllostomidae.

children.name: Uroderma bakeri
children.description: Uroderma bakeri — a species in the genus Uroderma, family Phyllostomidae. Also known as new world leaf-nosed bats.

children.name: Uroderma bilobatum
children.description: The tent-making bat is an American leaf-nosed bat (Phyllostomidae) found in lowland forests of Central and South America. This medium-sized bat has a gray coat with a pale white stripe running down the middle of the back. Its face is characterized by a fleshy nose-leaf and four white stripes.

children.description: Uroderma convexum — a species in the genus Uroderma, family Phyllostomidae. Also known as new world leaf-nosed bats.

children.name: Uroderma davisi
children.description: Uroderma davisi — a species in the genus Uroderma, family Phyllostomidae. Also known as new world leaf-nosed bats.

children.name: Uroderma magnirostrum
children.description: The brown tent-making bat is a species of bat found in South and Central America.

children.name: Choeroniscus
children.description: Choeroniscus — a genus of new world leaf-nosed bats in the family Phyllostomidae.

children.name: Choeroniscus godmani
children.description: Godman's long-tailed bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Guyana, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Suriname, and Venezuela.

children.name: Choeroniscus intermedins
children.description: Choeroniscus intermedins — a species in the genus Choeroniscus, family Phyllostomidae. Also known as new world leaf-nosed bats.

children.name: Choeroniscus minor
children.description: The lesser long-tongued bat, also called the lesser long-tailed bat, is a bat species from South America.

children.name: Choeroniscus periosus
children.description: The greater long-tailed bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae. It is found in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Sturnira bogotensis
children.description: The Bogotá yellow-shouldered bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae. It is found in Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela at altitudes from 300 m to above 2000 m, particularly in cloud forest. The species is primarily frugivorous; it may also consume nectar and pollen.

children.description: The highland yellow-shouldered bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Guyana, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, and Venezuela.

children.name: Sturnira luisi
children.description: Sturnira luisi — a species in the genus Sturnira, family Phyllostomidae. Also known as new world leaf-nosed bats.

children.name: Sturnira magna
children.description: The greater yellow-shouldered bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.

children.name: Sturnira mistratensis
children.description: The Mistratoan yellow-shouldered bat, is a species of leaf-nosed bat indigenous to the Cordillera Occidental, in the Mistrato municipality in Risaralda, Colombia. Since existing information derives exclusively from the holotype, the status of the species, its environmental requirements, and the trend of the population are unknown.

children.name: Sturnira mordax
children.description: The Talamancan yellow-shouldered bat is a species of bat in the family Phyllostomidae. It is found only in Costa Rica and Panama, and there are no subspecies.

children.name: Sturnira nana

children.description: Tschudi's yellow-shouldered bat is a species of leaf-nosed bat indigenous to Argentina, Ecuador, and Peru, with its range also encompassing Bolivia.

children.description: Sturnira perla is a species of yellow-shouldered bat found in Ecuador.

children.name: Sturnira sorianoi
children.description: Soriano's yellow-shouldered bat, is an extant species of leaf-nosed bat indigenous to Bolivia and Venezuela, although its precise distribution is uncertain. Following the postulation of the species in 2005, S. sorianoi requires contemporary information relating its distribution, environment, and population, along with formal a comparison with S.

children.description: Gardnerycteris crenulatum — a species in the genus Gardnerycteris, family Phyllostomidae. Also known as new world leaf-nosed bats.
